title: corydalis rutifolia subsp. erdelii
intro: Corydalis rutifolia subsp. erdelii, also known as Himalayan corydalis or Erdel's corydalis, is a plant species belonging to the family Papaveraceae, native to the Himalayan region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant contains compounds such as tetrahydroprotoberberine alkaloids, which have been found to have neuroprotective and cardiovascular health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat conditions such as pain, anxiety, and insomnia.
